resources.title: .cursorrules 파일 설정

resources.command: 프로젝트 루트 → .cursorrules 파일 생성 → 'TypeScript 필수, any 금지, 함수형 컴포넌트만, 한국어 주석' 등 규칙 작성

quiz.question: What is the purpose of the .cursorrules file in a project?
options: To define coding style, technologies used, and prohibited patterns
To store user preferences for the Cursor editor
To save project files in a specific format
To create backups of the project automatically
quiz.explanation: The .cursorrules file is used to define coding style, technologies, and prohibited patterns to enhance AI consistency in the project.

quiz.question: Which shortcut opens the Composer feature in Cursor?
quiz.explanation: Ctrl+I is the shortcut to open the Composer feature in Cursor, allowing users to generate code using natural language commands.

quiz.question: What does the Composer feature allow you to do?
options: Generate a Next.js + Tailwind to-do app from natural language commands
quiz.explanation: The Composer feature allows users to generate a Next.js + Tailwind to-do app from natural language commands, streamlining the coding process.

practicePrompts.label: Create a Todo App
practicePrompts.instruction: Generate a basic Next.js Todo app using natural language.
practicePrompts.prompt: Use Ctrl+I to open Composer and create a Next.js Todo app with Tailwind CSS.
practicePrompts.followUp: Add a feature to filter completed tasks.

practicePrompts.label: Set Coding Rules
practicePrompts.instruction: Define coding rules for a project using .cursorrules.
practicePrompts.prompt: Create a .cursorrules file to set coding style, technologies, and prohibited patterns for your project.
practicePrompts.followUp: Adjust the rules to include specific naming conventions.

practicePrompts.label: Explore Community Tips
practicePrompts.instruction: Find and apply a community tip for using Cursor effectively.
practicePrompts.prompt: Search for Cursor community tips and implement one to enhance your coding workflow.
practicePrompts.followUp: Share your own tip based on your experience with Cursor.
